fundamentals.name: ServiceNow, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: ServiceNow, Inc. provides cloud-based solution for digital workflows in the North America, Europe, the Middle East and Africa, Asia Pacific, and internationally. The company provides asset management, integrated risk management, IT service management, Operational Technology management, Security Operations, strategic portfolio management, IT operations management products; customer service management product; field service management applications; and sales and order management services. It also offers human resources delivery; legal and contract operations; workplace service delivery products; app engine product; automation engine; platform privacy and security product; and source-to-pay operations. In addition, the company provides RaptorDB, a database built to manage workloads at scale; ServiceNow Impact that provides customers with software tools, guided plans, and AI-driven recommendations; customer support; and workflow data fabric. It serves government, financial services, healthcare and life science, manufacturing, Public Sector, retail, technology, and Telecom sectors through service providers and resale partners. The company has a strategic collaboration with Cohesity, Inc. to develop, operate, and safeguard autonomous AI agents and data with enterprise-grade reliability. The company was formerly known as Service-now.com and changed its name to ServiceNow, Inc. in May 2012. ServiceNow, Inc. was founded in 2004 and is headquartered in Santa Clara, California.

judgment.summary: ServiceNow is a high-growth, profitable software company with strong margins and a solid free cash flow, trading at a premium valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter (ending 2026-03-31) reported EPS of $0.97, in line with estimates. The next earnings date is scheduled for 2026-07-22.
positives: Revenue growth 22.1% YoY, with analyst estimates for the current fiscal year at 17.5%.
Operating margin of 13.3% and profit margin of 12.6% indicate strong profitability.
Free cash flow of $5.11B TTM supports the business and potential shareholder returns.
Return on equity at 16.1% demonstrates efficient use of shareholder capital.
Forward P/E of 21.63 is significantly lower than trailing P/E of 64.72, suggesting growth expectations are more reasonably priced.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 64.72 and Price/Sales of 8.03 indicate a high valuation.
Current ratio of 0.84 and quick ratio of 0.69 suggest potential short-term liquidity concerns.
Earnings growth of 2.3% YoY is modest compared to revenue growth.
Debt/Equity ratio of 20.73, while offset by strong cash and FCF, warrants monitoring.

news.paragraph: ServiceNow (NOW) is trading at $108.73, with recent headlines indicating a mixed market sentiment. While some tech stocks are surging, Barron's suggests Nvidia is now a chip laggard, and other articles highlight gains in Palantir, Microsoft, and Red Cat. The broader market context shows tech stocks rising, partly driven by Snowflake's significant deal with Amazon and strong earnings. However, there's no specific news directly impacting ServiceNow's immediate price action in the provided articles.

headlines.title: Nvidia Stock Is Now a Chip Laggard
Why Palantir Stock Is Soaring Today
Why Microsoft Stock Is Surging Today
Why Red Cat Stock Is Skyrocketing Today
Stock Market Today, May 28: Tech Stocks Rise as Snowflake Surges After $6 Billion Amazon Deal and Strong Earnings
